summary.intent: Fix five Codex review thread issues on PR #724 in the ars-ui repo related to ARIA form accessibility, then push changes and trigger a new Codex review pass while monitoring CI.
summary.outcome: All five Codex threads were addressed in commit 363607c3, replied to, and resolved. A CI failure in Dioxus SSR field tests was subsequently discovered and fixed by correcting test child order in a follow-up commit.

repo: Dioxus SSR requires Description components to be registered before Input in raw/low-level composition — post-input Description cannot retroactively affect already-rendered input attributes.
aria-describedby on Field inputs must come from machine state only, not unconditionally synthesized description-id values.
Dioxus Fieldset Description registers during render behind a stable guard rather than waiting for an effect.
FieldProps.errors must be Vec<ars_forms::validation::Error> to match implementation and the shared field/form validation contract.
ARIA-mode invalid submit status resolves through adapter provider helpers, not hard-coded English strings.
The arbitrary raw child-order limitation in Fieldset composition is a known user-approved tradeoff; owned TextField-style components will have enough upfront structure to avoid order constraints.

code.finding: Test `field_renders_root_label_input_and_messages` had Description placed after Input; reordering to Description-before-Input fixed the SSR rendering assertion.

workflow: Codex review is triggered by posting '@codex review' as a PR comment and monitored via GitHub reactions (👀 = in progress) and unresolved thread count via GraphQL.
CI job logs are fetched via `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/jobs/{job_id}/logs` to diagnose failures.
The review loop polls every ~120 seconds checking both Codex reaction state and open thread count before deciding next action.
Inline Codex thread replies reference the fixing commit hash for traceability before resolving via GraphQL mutation.

friction: Commit hooks caused a delay before the push could proceed, requiring repeated polling via write_stdin.
CI adapter test failure appeared only after the main fix was pushed, requiring a second diagnostic and fix cycle.
The Dioxus SSR child-order constraint is an inherent low-level composition limitation that surfaces as test fragility.
open_items: Owned TextField-style components need to be implemented to eliminate the raw child-order constraint in Fieldset composition.
Longer CI jobs (adapter, feature, unit, release, coverage) were still pending at session end — final CI status unconfirmed.
A fresh Codex review pass was triggered but had not yet produced findings or approval at session end.
